Description.Name: Historical Vengeance
Description.Details: Fourteen days since the convoy incident. The Argo has been running standard operations in the meantime, focusing on a short return to the more normal. Suddenly the HPG relay activates. Incoming transmission using the Tribe's encryption, with priority flagging. Darius brings it up on the main display. Joanna McEvedy appears, but something has changed. Her expression is colder, harder. Even less patience than usual in her eyes, much more steel. "Commander {COMMANDER.Callsign}. The re-verification of all my intelligence agents is finished. My network has been secured and purged of Ghost Bear infiltration." She leans forward slightly, intensity radiating from the screen. "But the time for careful, surgical strikes has passed. Ghost Bear Watch has escalated their operations across the entire Periphery. They're not just hunting warriors anymore. They're targeting families. Elders. Children. Anyone with Wolverine blood or Tribe connections." "They're doing to us what they did in 2824 when they annihilated Clan Wolverine. Systematic extermination disguised as counter-terrorism. We will not allow history to repeat itself." Her voice drops to something darker, more zealous. "They tried to erase us from history. They hunted our ancestors to extinction. Now we'll dismantle their capacity to hunt us. Every Ghost Bear institution that has participated in operations against Minnesota Tribe will answer for centuries of persecution. This is justice for all the lost Wolverines of the past." "I'm attaching our latest intelligence package now. Verified through multiple independent sources. You'll see the pattern of Ghost Bear escalation across the Periphery." Farah pauses the transmission and pulls up the incoming data package. "Commander, confirming data reception. Large intelligence file, multiple operational summaries, prisoner manifests, facility locations." She scans through the files quickly. "Initial review shows consistency with Joanna's claims about Ghost Bear operations. Reports of raids on suspected Tribe locations, detention of civilians, increased Watch activity in frontier systems." She looks up, expression cautious. "But I'll need time to verify this independently. Cross-reference with public records, compare with known Ghost Bear deployment patterns. We should be certain of its accuracy before we act." Darius nods. "Good. Resume the transmission. Let's hear what she wants." Farah reactivates the feed. Joanna continues without pause. "Our first priority is immediate. Ghost Bear Watch maintains a secret detention facility in the St. Andreas system. They're holding Minnesota Tribe operatives captured during recent operations." She pulls up a prisoner manifest on her display. "Two confirmed Tribe personnel. Pilots, intelligence officers, logistics coordinators. Our people. They've been classified as 'terrorists and war criminals' by Ghost Bear command. They're still under trial, but we don't expect a fair ruling and their execution will occur soon." "We will not abandon our own to Ghost Bear justice. You are to transit to St. Andreas, breach the detention facility, and extract all Minnesota Tribe operatives. Secondary objective is to extract all prisoners flagged as 'Tribe-affiliated' by our intelligence network." Facility schematics appear on the transmission. "Expected opposition is heavy. Heavy and assault mechs highly likely, several lances worth. The facility is hardened and prepared for potential rescue attempts. This will be a quite difficult operation. I will send some support units to rendezvous with you during the mission. This means you need to immediately deploy on arrival, or they will expedite the execution as soon as they spot our forces moving in." Her voice turns to ice. "This facility has already held our people for months. They've been interrogated, tortured, psychologically broken. Ghost Bear 'justice' means eventual execution for anyone with Wolverine connections, but never a fast end to the suffering. We extract our own, Commander. Always. Bring them home." The transmission pauses. Darius immediately pulls up the mission data. "Boss, this is heavily defended. Prison break operations are always high-risk, but this one is serious. We're looking at assault-class opposition in a fortified position." Yang reviews the facility schematics. "Fortified position, overlapping turret coverage, prepared defensive positions. They're expecting a rescue attempt. Someone tipped them off or they're just paranoid. Either way, this won't be easy." Sumire analyzes the prisoner manifest. "Two Minnesota Tribe operatives with documented capture records. Names, ranks, specializations all listed. But there's a secondary list of those three prisoners flagged as 'Tribe-affiliated.' The identifications on these seem less certain. They are marked as 'suspected troublemakers' or 'possible Tribe sympathizers.'" Joanna's voice cuts back in. "Extract everyone marked on that manifest, Commander. We cannot leave potential allies or sympathizers to Ghost Bear interrogation. They'll break anyone eventually." The crew exchanges glances. No one speaks, but the look is clear. Something feels different about this. Sumire pulls up system data. "St. Andreas system. Single inhabited world, St. Timalin. Periphery colony world, population approximately four million. Primary terrain is temperate forests and grasslands with scattered urban centers." "The detention facility is located in a remote region, approximately two hundred kilometers from the nearest civilian settlement. Isolated by design for security purposes." "Historical note. St. Timalin was settled during the late Star League era. No significant military importance, primarily agricultural and light manufacturing economy." Darius looks to you. "Boss, rescuing prisoners of war is legitimate military action. Her own people are in that facility facing execution. But Joanna's tone has changed. She's talking about historical justice, repaying centuries-old debts. That's not the language of a defensive operation." Yang grunts. "Mission's still solid though. Extract captured operatives before they're executed. That's the job. Whatever Joanna's personal motivations are, the mission makes sense." Sumire calculates transit time. "St. Andreas is distant. We're looking at multiple jumps, several weeks of travel time. We should begin preparations immediately if we're accepting this contract." Farah still looks troubled but nods. "I'll continue verifying the intelligence package during transit. If there are discrepancies, I hope to detect them before we engage."

Description.Name: Prepare for transit to St. Andreas.
Prepare for transit to St. Andreas.
Description.Details: Darius logs the mission acceptance. "Understood, boss. Plotting jump sequence to St. Andreas now." Yang heads for the mech bay. "I'll start prepping for assault-class opposition. We're going to need heavy firepower to crack that facility." Sumire coordinates with navigation. "Jump calculations complete. First transit initiates in eight hours." The Argo begins preparations for the long transit to St. Andreas. [A new flashpoint has been created in the St. Andreas system to continue the campaign]
